title: cerastium viscosum var. glomeratum
intro: Cerastium viscosum var. glomeratum, also known as sticky mouse-ear or sticky chickweed, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have made it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Cerastium viscosum var. glomeratum can be prepared as an infusion or a tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
